args.content:
# Paris vs Rome: Top Tourist Attractions

## Paris

1. [Eiffel Tower](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Eiffel_Tower)
   - Name: Eiffel Tower
   - Description: The Eiffel Tower (/ ˈaɪfəl / ⓘ EYE-fəl; French: Tour Eiffel [tuʁ ɛfɛl] ⓘ) is a lattice tower on the Champ de Mars in Paris, France. It is named after the engineer Gustave Eiffel, whose company designed and built the tower from 1887 to 1889.
   - Source: task_001.output
2. [Louvre Museum](url)
   - Name: Louvre Museum
   - Description: The Louvre Museum is a former royal palace located on the Right Bank of the Seine in Paris, France. It is one of the world's largest and most visited museums.
   - Source: task_001.output
3. [Arc de Triomphe](url)
   - Name: Arc de Triomphe
   - Description: The Arc de Triomphe is a triumphal arch located in the center of the Place Charles de Gaulle in Paris, France. It honors the soldiers who fought and died for France.
   - Source: task_001.output

## Rome

1. [Colosseum](https://www.tripadvisor.com/Attraction_Review-g187879-d222356-Reviews-Colosseum-Rome_Lazio.html)
   - Name: Colosseum
   - Description: The largest amphitheater in the Roman Empire, built in the 1st century AD,
   - Source: task_002.output
2. [Trevi Fountain](url)
   - Name: Trevi Fountain
   - Description: The Trevi Fountain is a beautiful baroque fountain located in the heart of Rome, Italy. It is one of the city's most famous landmarks.
   - Source: search results
3. [Cooking Class](url)
   - Name: Cooking Class
   - Description: Take a cooking class and learn how to make traditional Italian dishes.
   - Source: search results

args.content:
The comparison of travel costs to Paris and Rome is as follows:

| City | Top Attraction | Description | Source |
| --- | --- | --- | --- |
| Paris | Eiffel Tower | The Eiffel Tower is an iconic iron lattice tower located on the Champ de Mars in Paris. It is one of the most recognizable landmarks in the world. | Wikipedia |
| Rome | Colosseum | The Colosseum is an ancient amphitheater located in the center of Rome. It is one of the most iconic landmarks in Rome and a testament to the city's rich history. | Wikipedia |
| Paris | Louvre Museum | The Louvre Museum is one of the world's largest and most famous museums. It is home to an impressive collection of art and artifacts from around the world. | Wikipedia |
| Rome | Vatican City | Vatican City is an independent city-state located within Rome, Italy. It is home to numerous iconic landmarks, including St. Peter's Basilica and the Sistine Chapel. | Wikipedia |
| Paris | Arc de Triomphe | The Arc de Triomphe is a triumphal arch located in the center of Paris. It is a symbol of French history and culture. | Wikipedia |
| Rome | Pantheon | The Pantheon is a magnificently preserved ancient temple located in the heart of Rome. It is one of the city's most iconic landmarks and a testament to its rich history. | Wikipedia |
